title: Theme: Embracing God's Grace in Daily Life
content:
Good afternoon! As we navigate the busyness of life here in Nairobi on this February day in 2026, let's reflect on the profound truth found in Ephesians 2:8-9: "For it is by grace you have been saved, through faith—and this is not from yourselves, it is the gift of God—not by works, so that no one can boast."
Brothers and sisters, grace is not just a distant theological concept; it's the very air we breathe as followers of Christ. In a world filled with challenges—economic pressures, personal struggles, or the uncertainties of tomorrow—it's easy to fall into the trap of self-reliance. We think, "If I just work harder, pray more, or do better, then God will bless me." But today's message is a reminder that God's grace is unearned and abundant. It's His gift that covers our shortcomings and empowers us to live victoriously.
Consider the story of the prodigal son in Luke 15. He squandered his inheritance, hit rock bottom, and returned home expecting judgment. Instead, his father ran to him with open arms, robes, and a feast. That's grace in action—not based on merit, but on love. Today, God is extending that same grace to you. Whether you're facing a difficult decision at work, strained relationships, or doubts about your faith, lean into His unmerited favor.
How do we embrace this grace daily?

Surrender your efforts: Stop striving in your own strength. Start your day by acknowledging, "Lord, I need Your grace today."
Extend grace to others: Just as you've received it freely, give it to those around you—forgive quickly, love unconditionally.
Live in gratitude: Thank God for His gifts, big and small, and watch how it transforms your perspective.

Let this grace fuel your actions today. Go forth knowing you're not defined by your failures but by His endless love. May the peace of Christ guard your hearts and minds. Amen. If you'd like a prayer or more on this theme, just let me know!

title: Theme: Enduring Trials: Refined Like Gold
content:
Good morning, on this bright March 2, 2026! As we start the day, let's draw strength from God's Word. According to Bible Gateway's Verse of the Day, today's verse is Job 23:10-11 (NIV):
"But he knows the way that I take; when he has tested me, I will come forth as gold. My feet have closely followed his steps; I have kept to his way without turning aside."
etsy.cometsy.com

Brothers and sisters, this verse comes from the book of Job, where a righteous man faces unimaginable suffering—loss of family, health, and wealth. Yet, in his pain, Job declares his confidence in God's sovereignty. He likens trials to a refiner's fire, where gold is purified by heat, emerging stronger and more valuable. God knows our paths intimately; He allows tests not to break us, but to refine us.
In our daily lives here in Kenya, we encounter our own "fires"—perhaps financial hardships, health issues, or the stresses of city life in Nairobi. It's tempting to question God or stray from His ways. But this teaching reminds us: Stay faithful. Follow His steps diligently, and trust that these challenges are shaping you into something precious.
Practical steps to apply this today:

Reflect on your path: Take a moment in prayer to acknowledge God's knowledge of your journey.
Persevere in faith: When faced with a trial, remember the outcome—emerging as gold.
Support others: Encourage someone going through tough times, reminding them of God's refining process.

May this truth empower you to face the day with renewed hope. God's got you, and He'll bring you through shining brighter. Amen. If you need more on this or a personal prayer, I'm here!
